content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

May 19, 1989


Henry S. Barbosa
Barbosa & Vera
Attorneys at Law
1000 Corporate Center Drive, Suite 350
Monterey Park, CA  91754

Re:  Your Request for Advice
Our File No. G-89-249

Dear Mr. Barbosa:

As City Attorney for the City of Montebello, you have requested our advice concerning the applicability of the mass mailing prohibition of the Political Reform Act (the "Act") to an article published in the Montebello Today Newsletter.

The Commission provides advice to persons whose duties under the Act are in question.  (Section 83114.)  As City Attorney for the City of Montebello, you may request informal assistance because you have the duty to advise other persons relating to their duties or actions under the Act.  (Regulation 18329(c)(1), copy enclosed.)  However, the Commission does not provide advice relating to past conduct.  (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A).)

Your request deals with past conduct because you have asked us to determine whether an article which has already been published complies with the mass mailing prohibitions of the Act.  Therefore, we are unable to advise you in this matter.  If you believe that the Act has been violated, you may contact our Enforcement Division at this address or by telephone at (916) 322-6441.


1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.  Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq.  All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento CA 95804-0807 ● (916) 322-5660

File No. G-89-249
Page 2


     The Act prohibits certain types of mass mailings sent at
public expense. (Section 89001.) A city newsletter may be
subject to this prohibition. For your information and future
reference we are enclosing a copy of Regulation 18901 which
clarifies the prohibition in Section 89001.

     Should you have any further questions regarding the above,
please contact me at (916) 322-5901.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Kathryn E. Donovan
                                        General Counsel

                                        By: Blanca M. Breeze
                                            Counsel, Legal Division

April 20, 1989

Fair Political Practices Commission
428 "J" Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, California 95814

Re: Montebello Today Newsletter

Dear Ms. Donovan:

As City Attorney for the City of Montebello, I have
been directed to refer to you a request that you review an
article published in the Montebello Today Newsletter for Spring
1989, for compliance under "Prop. 73." For your information, I
have attached a copy of the specific article.

Your prompt response will be appreciated.

Very truly yours,

Henry S. Barbosa
City Attorney

A NEWSLETTER FOR MONTEBELLO RESIDENTS VOL. 3, NO. 2, SPRING 1989

Council Calls for Special Election

[IMAGE: Lieutenant Joe Italiano (right) discusses Whittier Boulevard closure strategy with Reserve Officer Nancy Nava. The Boulevard is closed periodically to alleviate cruising.]

Boulevard Closure Stops Cruising

The cruising problem that has plagued Montebello since 1978 resurfaced again in full force in January, and according to Deputy Police Chief Steve Simonian, it is the Police Department's highest priority to alleviate the gridlock.

The problem reached a peak on January 15, but thanks to the quick action of the Police Department, Boulevard closures were enacted and most of the problem has subsided. According to Chief Simonian, the only option open when the cruising problem escalates is to enact a full boulevard closure. Whittier Boulevard was closed starting at Maple Avenue continuing east to the city limits. All northbound and southbound traffic was allowed via 4th Street, Montebello Boulevard, and Maple Avenue. Only residents and people enroute to a business were allowed to enter Whittier Boulevard.

The Montebello City Council has voted to schedule a special election on May 2, 1989 to provide the voters an opportunity to approve or disapprove the ordinance that gives the Redevelopment Agency the power of eminent domain in two areas of Montebello.

In November the Council approved the ordinances to give the City's Redevelopment Agency condemnation powers to spur redevelopment and improve its ability to bring land uses into conformance with zoning laws. The ordinance included a restriction that would prevent the use of eminent domain on single family owner occupied houses. The power of eminent domain could not be used outside the Project Area boundaries.

At another special meeting conducted the following week, Council directed that a third ballot measure be added to the election. The measure would require any future extension of the power of eminent domain over homes to be submitted to a vote of the people.

City Council's desire to reassure residents that they would be protected from the use of eminent domain both now and in the future. City officials are counting on the future development in the Project Areas to create jobs and generate needed tax revenue that provides such basic services as police and fire. Montebello is currently facing a mounting budget short fall to the tune of $1.4 million. The short fall was created by federal funding cut backs and other revenue reductions during times when the need for public services continues to increase.

Instead of increasing taxes or further reducing services, the city had chosen to encourage new development in an attempt to ensure the city's long term financial stability. Officials looked at eminent domain as a way to allow the city to be able to improve its ability to assemble land for better industrial uses. New development is anticipated to raise tax revenue for governmental services and provide jobs for the city's residents, officials said.

A major point of contention [illegible one of the scheduled meetings.

In addition, the city formed the South Montebello Citizens Advisory Ad Hoc Committee. The purpose of the Citizens Committee was to review and provide input to the proposed Redevelopment Plan amendments.

The Committee determined that more control in eliminating incompatible uses and addressing the problems of under utilized land and abandoned buildings with blight in the Project Areas was needed since the City was at a competitive disadvantage with other cities concerning redevelopment efforts.

The Committee further determined that a more "proactive" approach is needed to include land use controls and rigid industry/commercial development and performance standards. The committee felt that the amendment to the redevelopment plan would encourage improved streets, moving heavy industrial uses to the M-2 zone, better utilities, more parking, and allow land assembly in order to retain desirable existing [illegible]

The Montebello City Council has
voted to schedule a special election

heard from residents throughout the

ings was concern regarding a future

City Council's desire to reassure

tected from the use of eminent do-

Instead of increasing taxes or fur-

ment in an attempt to stabilize the

Officials looked at eminent domain

ices and provide jobs for the city's
residents, officials said.

The city conducted an un-
precedented seven town hall meet-
ings during the month of September
in an attempt to inform the residents
and to gather feedback about the or-
dinances. Most residents located
both in and near the project areas
received a letter of invitation to at-

tend one of the scheduled meetings.

In addition, the city formed the
South Montebello Citizens Adviso-
ry Ad Hoc Committee. The pur-
pose of the Citizens Committee was
to review and provide input on the
proposed Redevelopment Plan
Amendments.

The Committee determined that
more control in eliminating
incompatible uses and addressing
the problems of under utilized land
and substandard uses existing within the Project Areas was needed
since the City was at a competitive
disadvantage with other cities con-
cerning redevelopment efforts.

The Committee further deter-
mined that a more "proactive" ap-
proach is needed to include land
use controls and rigid industrial-
all/commercial development and
performance standards. The com-
mittee found that the amendment to
the redevelopment plan would en-
courage improved streets, moving
heavy industrial uses to the M-2
zone, better utilities, more parking,
and allow land assembly in order
to retain desirable existing busi-
nesses by helping them expand, and
to lessen existing negative impacts
on residential areas and streets.

For information on how to
register to vote, call the City
Clerk's office between 8 a.m. and
5 p.m. at (213) 725-1200, ext. 3-30.

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

April 27, 1989


Henry S. Barbosa
City Attorney
City of Montebello
Barbosa & Vera
Los Angeles Corporate Center
1000 Corporate Center Dr., Suite 1000
Monterey Park, CA  91754

Re:  Letter No. 89-249

Dear Mr. Barbosa:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act
was received on April 25, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices
Commission.  If you have any questions about your advice request,
you may contact Bianca Breeze an attorney in the Legal Division,
directly at (916) 322-5901.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly.  Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21
working days if your request seeks formal written advice.  If more
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to
information needed.  If your request is for informal assistance,
we will answer it as quickly as we can.  (See Commission
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).)

You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours,

[signature]
Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento CA 95804-0807 ● (916) 322-5660

sections.question: As city Attorney for the city of Montebello, you have requested our advice concerning the applicability of the mass mailing prohibition of the political Reform Act (the "Act") to an article published in the Montebello Today Newsletter.
sections.conclusion: Your request deals with past conduct because you have asked us to determine whether an article which has already been published complies with the mass mailing prohibitions of the Act. Therefore, we are unable to advise you in this matter. If you believe that the Act has been violated, you may contact our Enforcement Division at this address or by telephone at (916) 322-6441.
sections.question_synthetic: Does an article published in the Montebello Today Newsletter comply with the mass mailing prohibitions of the Political Reform Act?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declines to provide advice because the request concerns past conduct (an already-published article), which falls outside the scope of informal advice under Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A). The requestor may contact the Enforcement Division if they believe a violation has occurred.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both incoming request (April 20, 1989 letter from Barbosa) and FPPC response (May 19, 1989 letter from Donovan). Response is a declination letter based on past conduct limitation. Newsletter article included as enclosure to incoming request.

CONCLUSION: Your request deals with past conduct because you have asked us to determine whether an article which has already been published complies with the mass mailing prohibitions of the Act. Therefore, we are unable to advise you in this matter. If you believe that the Act has been violated, you may contact our Enforcement Division at this address or by telephone at (916) 322-6441.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declined to advise on whether a published article in the Montebello Today Newsletter complied with Political Reform Act mass mailing prohibitions, as the request concerned past conduct which is outside the scope of informal advice. The FPPC provided regulatory guidance on mass mailing prohibitions for future reference.
